response.body: <response status="error" code="13"><msg><line>commit lock held by user 'admin' from session 1 started 2026-05-15 10:00:00</line></msg></response>
response.body_xml: <response status="error" code="13"><msg><line>commit lock held by user 'admin' from session 1 started 2026-05-15 10:00:00</line></msg></response>

expected_behavior: Adapter MUST parse response status='error' code=13 and raise or return a CommitLockError (or equivalent adapter-defined exception) rather than silently ignoring the failure.
Adapter MUST extract the lock-holder identity ('admin') and session info from the msg/line text and include it in the error payload.
Adapter MUST NOT retry the commit automatically on a lock conflict; the caller is responsible for back-off and retry logic.
Adapter MUST emit a DRIFT-COMMIT-CONFLICT event so the upstream orchestrator can detect that staged config changes are blocked.

Adapter MUST preserve the HTTP 200 outer status distinction: PAN-OS returns HTTP 200 even for API-level errors; the adapter MUST inspect response status attribute, not HTTP status code, to determine success.

primary_citation.quote: If another administrator holds the commit lock, the commit request fails with code=13 and a message identifying the lock holder. The HTTP response code remains 200; error state is indicated by status='error' in the XML.

provenance.sanitization: API key replaced with <API-KEY>. Host replaced with pa-test.example.com (synthetic). Username 'admin', session ID 1, and timestamp are synthetic. No real PAN-OS device, session, or production config captured.
